dashboard.label: Getting Started
dashboard.description: Complete the following steps to configure your environment for Alpha Level. Once finished, verify all checks pass on the Readiness dashboard.

div.p: This add-on configures your Splunk Cloud environment for Alpha Level managed alert scoring. Alpha Level retrieves notable events over the management port, scores them externally, and returns results via HEC. Configuration typically takes 15 minutes.

By default, scored results and heartbeats are stored in index=main. For separation, create dedicated alphalevel_results and alphalevel_internal indexes in the Splunk Cloud Admin Console, then override these macros in local/macros.conf:
Create a HEC token (Settings → Data Inputs → HTTP Event Collector) that accepts sourcetypes alphalevel:result and alphalevel:heartbeat into the indexes configured above.

Create a dedicated service account and grant it the alphalevel_svc role shipped with this TA. Then create a long-TTL Splunk auth token bound to that service account (Settings → Tokens). Alpha Level uses this token to retrieve events over the management port.
div.h3: 4. Set up integration in Alpha Level
div.p:
Log in to the Alpha Level admin portal at
admin.alphalevel.ai
and create a new integration. You will need:

If your alerts live somewhere other than index=notable, override the alphalevel_custom_sources macro in local/macros.conf. Also extend srchIndexesAllowed on the alphalevel_svc role in local/authorize.conf to include those indexes.
Once Alpha Level enables the puller for your tenant, open the Readiness dashboard. All checks should pass within 5-15 minutes.
